fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Low-cost index fund tracking midcap stocks. Midcaps have higher growth potential than large caps over long periods. 0.2% yearly fee keeps costs low. Tax-efficient (equity-taxed). Fits your high equity comfort.

fund.name: Nippon India Nifty Smallcap 250 Index Fund - Direct Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: Tracks 250 smallest listed companies. Smallcaps offer highest long-term growth potential but are volatile. 0.2% yearly fee is very low. Equity-taxed. Matches your aggressive risk appetite and satellite strategy.

fund.name: BANDHAN SMALL CAP FUND - DIRECT PLAN GROWTH

allocations.rationale: Actively managed smallcap fund with strong 3-year returns. Adds diversification beyond index exposure. Higher 1.2% yearly fee is justified by consistent performance. Equity-taxed. Suits your preference for smallcap satellite.

fund.name: Nippon India Large Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: High-performing largecap fund with consistent 5-year returns. Provides stability to the portfolio. 1% yearly fee is standard. Equity-taxed. Balances the higher-risk mid and small cap funds.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Hybrid fund with ~70% in stocks and rest in bonds. Reduces overall portfolio volatility while staying equity-taxed. 1% yearly fee. Helps manage risk during market swings. Good for lump sum deployment when ashore.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera (takes 5 minutes, needs PAN and bank). Step 2: Complete KYC if not already done. Step 3: Start your monthly SIPs of ₹30,000 across the five funds. Step 4: Transfer your ₹20 lakh existing corpus and invest as per lumpsum amounts. Step 5: Set calendar reminders for SIP dates.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if SIPs are running and lumpsum is invested.
After 1 year: Compare each fund's performance with its category average. If any fund underperforms for 2 consecutive years, consider switching.
Every 3 years: Review goal progress — see if you're on track to build ₹8Cr.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check your portfolio mix. If any category (large/mid/small/hybrid) is more than 5% away from target, sell excess and buy underweight parts. Example: If smallcaps grow to 55% of equity, sell some and move to large/mid/hybrid to restore balance.
plan.rationale: You're 40 with 20 years to retirement and aggressive risk appetite. This plan uses low-cost index funds for mid and small caps (65% of equity) to capture market growth cheaply, adds one strong active smallcap fund for satellite exposure, balances with largecap and hybrid for stability. ₹30,000/month SIP + ₹20L lumpsum aligns with your income pattern. All funds are equity-taxed, optimal under new tax regime. Targets ₹8Cr with room for education and house.
risks: Stock markets can drop 30-40% in bad years — your portfolio value will fall temporarily. This is normal.
Smallcap funds are more volatile — they may fall harder than large stocks in crashes.
Past returns don't guarantee future results. 17% CAGR is a projection, not a promise.
Lumpsum investment timing matters — investing all at a market peak may delay returns.
Fund managers can change, affecting performance.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: After 10 years, reduce equity exposure gradually
Closer to retirement, shift more to safer assets

aps.reasoning:
passive_instrument_fraction: 70% of the equity allocation is in index funds (Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 and Nippon India Nifty Smallcap 250), while 30% is in actively managed funds (Bandhan Small Cap, Nippon Large Cap, ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt). Since two out of five funds are passive and account for 50% of total allocation, but they represent 70% of the equity sleeve (50% out of 70% equity), the score is 0.7.

turnover_score: Rebalancing is recommended annually only if allocations deviate by more than 5%, and review checkpoints are at 6 months, 1 year, and every 3 years. This indicates moderate but not frequent turnover — not pure buy-and-hold, but not tactical trading either. Hence, 0.75.

cost_emphasis_score: The rationale explicitly mentions low expense ratios (0.2% for index funds) as a benefit and justifies higher fees only with performance. Cost is a key but not the sole criterion, so score is high but not perfect — 0.8.

research_vs_cost_score: The plan uses index funds for broad exposure (passive) but also selects active funds based on performance history and rationale (e.g., 'strong 3-year returns', 'consistent performance'). Some research is evident, but cost and indexing dominate. Score: 0.6.

time_horizon_alignment_score: The plan is designed for a 20-year horizon, with long-term compounding, SIP step-up, and gradual de-risking over time. Review and rebalancing are infrequent. Strong long-term orientation — 0.9.

portfolio_activeness_score: Two index funds have beta close to 1 and lower tracking error (though not very low — ~8-11%), while active funds show higher tracking error (11-12%) and positive alpha. However, the largecap fund has low tracking error (3.16%), suggesting closet indexing. Hybrid fund has moderate beta (0.66) and higher tracking error. Overall, the portfolio blends passive and active, leaning slightly toward passive due to significant index fund allocation and cost focus. Score: 0.65.

pqs.reasoning: The investment plan is highly aligned with Captain Haddock's aggressive risk appetite, 20-year horizon, and financial goals (₹8Cr corpus, house, education). The portfolio is heavily equity-weighted (85% initially), using a smart mix of index and active funds across large, mid, and small caps, plus an aggressive hybrid for stability and lump sum deployment. This provides strong diversification across market caps and fund houses, though slightly tilted toward small/mid caps, which is justified by the investor's risk profile. Projected returns of 17% CAGR are ambitious but plausible for this allocation; risks are clearly disclosed, including volatility and timing concerns. The plan is internally consistent: rationale, allocations, rebalancing, and de-risking schedule all support a long-term, high-growth strategy. Critically, all funds are equity-taxed (including the Aggressive Hybrid), which is optimal for a 'new_regime' investor who cannot claim 80C benefits and must minimize slab-based taxation. The plan avoids slab-taxed debt funds entirely, correctly leveraging equity tax advantages (LTCG up to ₹1.25L exempt, then 12.5%). ELSS is not included, which is appropriate under the new regime. Thus, tax efficiency is perfect.
